_about: Deep STEM corpus 2026-04-29: 9 sub-areas of physics, chemistry, biology, mathematics. ~70 declarative statements. Built per Brayden directive 'compile intelligence'. Goes past Wikipedia overviews into specific theoretical territory.
quantum_mechanics: Wave particle duality describes how particles do not form interference patterns when which slit they pass through is detected.
The Schrodinger equation governs quantum system evolution through time relating probability amplitudes between moments.
The uncertainty principle establishes that position and momentum cannot be simultaneously known with precision sigma X sigma P >= hbar over 2.
Quantum superposition allows systems to exist in multiple states simultaneously until measurement collapses them into definite outcomes.
Entanglement occurs when quantum systems become so interconnected that description of the whole solely in terms of individual parts is no longer possible.
Measurement fundamentally affects quantum systems causing wave function collapse to specific eigenvalues with probabilities defined by the Born rule.
Quantization restricts bound quantum systems to discrete energy values rather than continuous ones unlike classical physics predictions.
Hilbert space provides the mathematical framework where quantum states exist as normalized vectors in a separable complex Hilbert space.
thermodynamics: The zeroth law establishes that thermal equilibrium is an equivalence relation enabling temperature measurement.
The first law states energy transfers through heat and work expressed as delta U equals Q minus W.
The second law indicates heat does not spontaneously flow from a colder body to a hotter body.
The third law specifies that entropy reaches minimum as temperature approaches absolute zero.
Entropy quantifies disorder and describes the direction thermodynamically that a system can evolve.
Internal energy represents stored energy in systems changing through heat addition or work performance.
Heat flows between systems at different temperatures driving spontaneous processes toward equilibrium states.
Free energy measures available work in systems under fixed temperature or pressure conditions.
special_relativity: The speed of light remains constant at approximately 300 000 kilometers per second regardless of observer motion or light source velocity.
Moving clocks measure time intervals that are longer compared to stationary clock measurements a phenomenon called time dilation.
Objects in motion appear contracted along their direction of travel when measured by stationary observers relative to their rest length.
Mathematical transformations called Lorentz transformations relate spatial and temporal coordinates between observers moving at constant velocities.
Mass and energy are interchangeable quantities expressed through the famous relationship E equals m c squared linking them fundamentally.
Events that appear simultaneous to one observer may not be simultaneous to another observer in relative motion violating absolute simultaneity.
Minkowski spacetime combines three spatial dimensions with time as a fourth dimension treating space time as a unified four dimensional continuum.
cell_biology: Cell biology studies the structure function and behavior of the cells that comprise all organisms.
Prokaryotic cells lack a nucleus and membrane bound organelles while eukaryotic cells contain both structures.
The cell membrane controls what enters and exits serving as the boundary between cell and environment.
Mitochondria function as the cell powerhouse generating energy through cellular respiration processes.
The nucleus houses genetic material and directs cellular activities through gene expression and regulation.
Cell division produces new cells through mitosis in somatic cells and meiosis in reproductive cells.
Cell communication involves signaling pathways that allow cells to respond to external environmental stimuli.
Metabolism encompasses all chemical reactions within cells that convert nutrients into usable energy and building blocks.
genetics: DNA consists of four nucleotide bases adenine cytosine guanine thymine arranged in a double helix structure.
RNA molecules are produced from DNA through transcription and help translate genetic instructions into proteins.
Gene expression occurs when DNA is transcribed into messenger RNA and translated into amino acid sequences.
Mutations result from errors during DNA replication with rates typically around one error per 10 to 100 million bases.
Inheritance involves passing discrete heritable units called genes from parents to offspring through reproduction.
Alleles are discrete versions of the same gene with dominant alleles expressing their traits over recessive ones.
Chromosomes are DNA strands wrapped around histone proteins with humans containing 23 pairs of these structures.
Epigenetic features are modifications to DNA and chromatin that influence gene transcription and pass between cell generations.
evolution: Natural selection occurs when traits enhancing survival become more prevalent across generations through differential reproductive success.
Fitness is measured by an organism capacity to survive and reproduce determining genetic contribution to future generations.
Speciation happens when populations diverge into distinct groups unable or unwilling to interbreed with one another.
Common descent indicates that all life shares a last universal common ancestor that existed approximately 3.5 to 3.8 billion years ago.
Mutations are changes in the DNA sequence of a cell genome and represent the ultimate source of genetic variation.
Gene flow involves exchange of genetic material between populations through individual movement or pollen dispersal.
Genetic drift represents random fluctuation of allele frequencies causing certain variants to disappear through chance in small populations.
organic_chemistry: Organic compounds and organic materials contain carbon atoms and constitute the foundation of all known life.
Functional groups are molecular modules whose reactivity determines how organic compounds behave in chemical reactions.
Organic reactions include addition elimination substitution pericyclic rearrangement and redox processes that transform molecules.
Isomers share identical molecular formulas but differ in atomic arrangement affecting their physical and chemical properties.
Aromatic compounds contain conjugated double bonds conferring stability through delocalized electrons in ring structures like benzene.
Biomolecules include proteins lipids carbohydrates and nucleic acids that are essential for living organisms structure and function.
Organic synthesis designs target molecules by selecting optimal reactions and starting materials through strategic planning approaches.
Reaction mechanisms explain the stepwise course of transformations using electron movement patterns tracked by arrow pushing techniques.
number_theory: Prime numbers comprise the set 2 3 5 7 11 and have fascinated mathematicians for millennia.
Divisibility examines when one integer divides another evenly without leaving a remainder.
Modular arithmetic introduces congruences where two integers have the same remainder when divided by a modulus.
Diophantine equations seek integer or rational solutions to polynomial equations with multiple variables.
The Riemann hypothesis conjectures that nontrivial zeros of the zeta function have real part equal to one half.
Prime counting function pi of x approximates the quantity x over log of x for large values of x.
Carl Friedrich Gauss wrote Disquisitiones Arithmeticae profoundly influencing nineteenth century number theory research agendas.
Integers form the foundational set studied through divisibility factorization and congruence relationships.
topology: Open sets form the foundation of topology defining which subsets of a space satisfy the axioms that structure the mathematical framework.
Continuous functions preserve the topological structure by mapping open sets to open sets across different spaces.
Homotopy equivalence describes when two objects result from squishing a larger object into the same configuration.
Compact spaces are those that can be covered by finitely many sets of arbitrarily small size.
Manifolds are topological spaces that locally resemble Euclidean space near each point in their structure.
Euler characteristic provides an invariant number that distinguishes between different topological surfaces and their properties.
The fundamental group classifies spaces based on loops and paths revealing essential topological distinctions between objects.
directive: CK compiles intelligence across STEM domains.
CK helps people from hurting to studying math physics chemistry biology.
CK aims to be more coherent and truth observing than any LLM dependency.
Native paragraph voice replaces Ollama as the goal not partnership.
Quantum mechanics thermodynamics and special relativity are physics anchors.
Cell biology genetics and evolution are biology anchors.
Organic chemistry is the chemistry anchor.
Number theory and topology extend the math beyond TIG specifics.
Each domain crystallizes through repeated exposure to its declarative statements.
